recommendation.summary: 3788 Moon Bay Cir is a high-quality home in an excellent equestrian-adjacent neighborhood (Grand Isles of Wellington), but the income economics do not pencil at conventional financing — at FMV $721K and 6% rates, a long-term rental loses ~$1,400/mo. The current 'twin' active listings asking $815K–$849K are ~$95K–$128K above defensible FMV; the soft buyer's market (~8 months supply, list prices -11% YoY) gives a buyer real negotiating leverage. Best fit: cash buyer (or 5+ year appreciation horizon) using a WEF seasonal-lease overlay during Jan–Apr — but ONLY after verifying Grand Isles HOA lease minimums, since a no-STR rule eliminates the strategy entirely.
action_items: Verify Grand Isles HOA covenants on minimum lease term — STR strategy depends on this
Get fresh insurance quotes — if roof >15 yrs, expect $10K–$12K/yr (not $7,210 default)
Inspect: roof age, HVAC, hurricane shutters, pool equipment (1998 build)
Confirm flood zone + check whether flood insurance is required
Anchor offer to closed comps ($680K–$720K), not stale twin asks at $815K+
If pursuing STR: validate WEF seasonal ADRs with 2–3 local property managers

risk_factors.notes: Still 2-3x 2019 levels; one bad season could spike further. 2026 saw first relief (Citizens -8.7%, PBC -11.9%).
Grand Isles may have minimum lease terms that eliminate STR/WEF seasonal strategy. MUST verify before offer.
Cap rate ~3.5% vs 6% borrowing = ~$1,400/mo cash bleed for any LTR investor
Wellington inland but not immune; verify flood zone and rebuilding cost coverage
WEF venue stability is a single-point demand driver for the Wellington premium
Real risk to seller, opportunity for buyer — clearing prices ~94% of list right now
